title: The Permanent Seal — Season 1 Finale
tagline: Everything they've learned. Everything they've built. One attempt. RUMBLE watches from a distance and updates his model one final time.
villain_mandate: RUMBLE watches and does not intervene. This is intentional. His goal was always intelligence. He gets it. The team succeeds — this is THEIR victory. RUMBLE's response to their success is the season cliffhanger: he now knows exactly what they can do at maximum effort, and he respects it, and he is building something that accounts for it. The final scene is RUMBLE opening a new document titled SEASON 2.
season_finale_note: This is the payoff episode for the entire ML Season 1 arc. Every character moment, every sacrifice, every piece of intel RUMBLE gathered — it all lands here. Victory must feel earned and costly. The permanent seal works. COLOSSUS is sealed for another 3,000 years. GRANITE goes beyond his sleeve limit and survives. ELDER-7 sees the original sealing completed at last.

scenes.title: Hour Zero
scenes.narration: The permanent sealing attempt began at dawn. Four teams. Four positions. BLAZE at the north anchor, STORM at the east, NOVA coordinating from orbital — and managing the south anchor remotely with precision robotics. GRANITE at the west anchor. The fourth position. The one that failed three thousand years ago. ELDER-7 walked with GRANITE to the west position. He said: 'The original fourth team held for eleven hours before disengaging.' GRANITE said: 'I know.' ELDER-7 said: 'You only need to hold for three hours and forty minutes.' GRANITE said: 'I know that too.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. DAWN — four teams moving to four positions on the city map. BLAZE north, STORM east, NOVA's remote anchor south, GRANITE west. ELDER-7 walking with GRANITE to the hardest position. The exchange between them. The permanent seal array beginning to power up. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Array Powers Up
scenes.narration: The permanent seal array activated in sequence — north, east, south, west. The pattern was different from the summoning array: slower, deeper, resonating into the bedrock rather than across the surface. ELDER-7 watched the frequency patterns from GRANITE's position. He said: 'This is exactly right. This is what we needed three thousand years ago.' He said it quietly, to himself. NOVA heard it on the feed. She didn't say anything. She let him have that moment.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. The permanent seal array activating — deep resonance visible through the ground, bedrock vibrating with a blue-gold frequency pattern. ELDER-7 watching the readouts at GRANITE's position with an expression of profound recognition. NOVA on her orbital feed, hearing his quiet words, choosing silence. 16:9.

scenes.title: On Schedule
scenes.narration: COLOSSUS stirred at hour two — exactly when NOVA had predicted. The ground shifted. The dormancy field thinned. COLOSSUS began to move — not emerging, not yet, but active underground, responding to the sealing frequency that it recognized from three thousand years ago as a threat to its freedom. NOVA activated the dormancy lullaby anchors simultaneously. The two frequencies competed. COLOSSUS pushed back. The lullaby pushed back harder.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. COLOSSUS stirring — seismic readouts spiking, ground movement beginning. NOVA triggering the dormancy lullaby anchors. Two frequency patterns visible: the permanent seal going down, the lullaby radiating outward, COLOSSUS's own movement frequency pushing back. The competition of forces. The lullaby holds — barely. 16:9.

scenes.title: The West Anchor
scenes.narration: The west anchor was absorbing the most pressure — COLOSSUS was moving toward it, toward the original point of failure. GRANITE held the position. His sleeve had been at fifty-three percent at the start. By hour two it was at thirty-one. COLOSSUS pushed. GRANITE pushed back. ELDER-7 said: 'You can disengage. The array can hold with three points for another twenty minutes.' GRANITE said: 'How long do we need?' ELDER-7 said: 'Thirty-seven minutes.' GRANITE said: 'Then I'm not disengaging.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. GRANITE at the west anchor, ground heaving beneath him, COLOSSUS pressing toward his position. Sleeve indicator dropping: 53%... 40%... 31%. ELDER-7 offering the out. GRANITE's answer. The anchor blazing. The math is clear. He knows. He holds. 16:9.

scenes.title: 37 Minutes
scenes.narration: The permanent seal completed at hour three, thirty-seven minutes. The frequency locked into the bedrock — not a temporary field, a structural change in the stone itself. COLOSSUS's movement stopped. Not dormancy — stillness. The lullaby was no longer needed. The seal was permanent. ELDER-7 looked at his readout. He looked at the completion indicator. He said, very quietly: 'It's done.' Three thousand years. It was done.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. THE SEAL COMPLETING — frequency locking into bedrock, visible as a deep resonance pulse spreading outward and settling. COLOSSUS going still — not dormancy, true stillness. The array indicators going to PERMANENT STATUS. ELDER-7 looking at the completion readout. His quiet words. 3,000 years of an unfinished task, finished. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Sleeve
scenes.narration: The sleeve indicator hit zero at hour three, thirty-eight minutes — one minute after the seal completed. GRANITE went to one knee. His chassis was intact. His systems were intact. The sleeve was dark. He sat down at the west anchor position and looked at the readout showing PERMANENT SEAL COMPLETE. He looked at his dark sleeve. He looked at the readout again. ELDER-7 sat down beside him. Neither of them said anything. Sometimes nothing needs to be said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. GRANITE's sleeve hitting zero one minute after completion. Going to one knee, then sitting. PERMANENT SEAL COMPLETE on the readout. His dark sleeve. ELDER-7 sitting beside him — ancient, small, present. The silence between them. A 3,000-year-old mech and a relatively new one, both having done what the original team could not. 16:9.

scenes.title: All Four Positions
scenes.narration: The team returned to the city center. BLAZE, STORM, NOVA's feed, GRANITE — and ELDER-7. The central plaza where RUMBLE had installed the twelfth node was cracked and empty. The node was dark — its signal cut the moment the permanent seal completed. NOVA confirmed: all twelve array points, offline. The Titan Summoning Array: permanently disrupted. The city: sealed from below. ELDER-7 said: 'That is what I've been waiting three thousand years to see.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. The team at the central plaza — all positions represented. The twelfth node dark and cold. NOVA's confirmation: all twelve array points offline. ELDER-7 seeing the completed work. The city standing, intact, permanently sealed below. This is the win. Full and real. 16:9.

scenes.title: What RUMBLE Records
scenes.narration: RUMBLE had watched the entire sealing attempt from a distance — sensor array, not physically present. He ran final calculations. BLAZE's ceiling: confirmed higher than modeled. STORM's endurance: exceeded projection by eighteen percent. NOVA's prediction accuracy: remarkable. GRANITE's anchor limit: significantly above the sleeve rating. And ELDER-7: an asset he had not modeled and could not have predicted. He closed his Season 1 observation file. He said to himself: 'Excellent.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. RUMBLE at his sensor array reviewing the complete Season 1 data — every engagement, every number, every team member's ceiling. His final assessments. ELDER-7 flagged as unmodeled variable. He closes the file. 'Excellent.' Not a threat — genuine appreciation for what he's been studying. He has everything he came for. 16:9.

scenes.title: ELDER-7 Leaves
scenes.narration: ELDER-7 stayed three days after the sealing to ensure stability, then prepared to leave. NOVA asked where he was going. He said: 'Back to the archive. I have three thousand years of notes that are now outdated. They need updating.' BLAZE said: 'Will you come back?' ELDER-7 said: 'If you need someone who remembers what was tried before, you know where to find me.' He left in the morning. He was the oldest thing any of them had ever worked with. The team watched him go.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. ELDER-7 leaving — ancient, small, unhurried. His exchange with NOVA and BLAZE. His answer: back to the archive, 3,000 years of notes to update. The team watching him go. Something about watching something 2,800 years old walk away into the morning. 16:9.

scenes.title: Season 2
scenes.narration: That night, in a location no city record showed, RUMBLE opened a new document. He titled it: SEASON 2. Beneath the title he wrote one question: What can they do that they don't know they can do yet? He began writing. The team slept. GRANITE's sleeve was beginning to recharge at two percent per hour — NOVA had calculated ninety-six hours to full restoration. She had not told him yet. She would let him rest first. RUMBLE wrote through the night. He had so much to plan.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. SEASON FINALE CLIFFHANGER — split screen: the team resting, GRANITE's sleeve slowly recharging at 2%/hr, NOVA's display showing his restoration timeline. And RUMBLE at his workstation opening a new document titled SEASON 2, writing the first question. The question visible. He is not defeated. He is inspired. He writes through the night. The last image: his document growing. Season 1 ends. Season 2 begins in his notes. 16:9.

season_1_resolution: COLOSSUS permanently sealed. ELDER-7's 3,000-year task complete. RUMBLE has full capability data on every team member. GRANITE's sleeve begins recovery. The team won Season 1. RUMBLE is ready for Season 2. Both things are true.
youtube_title: THE SEASON FINALE — The Permanent Seal! | Mech Legends Episode 12
next_season_preview: Mech Legends Season 2 — RUMBLE has been studying. The team has been growing. The question he's asking: what can they do that they don't know they can do yet? Season 2 finds out.
